title: ReplayProtector HashMap grows unbounded as peers arrive
description: In rust/lichen-link/src/link_layer.rs:192-194, ReplayProtector uses a HashMap keyed by (PublicKey, epoch). Each new peer and epoch creates a new entry. There's no eviction policy. Over time, with many peers and epoch rollovers, this can exhaust memory. reset_peer exists but must be called explicitly. Consider: add a max_peers limit, LRU eviction, or periodic cleanup based on last-seen time.
